title: Persona Metrics — survival.jsonl row
description: One row per finding from the prior stage, judged by the survival classifier at the start of /plan, /check, or /build (Phase 0). The classifier runs in one of two modes selected by the calling command: addressed-by-revision (compares pre-snapshot vs revised artifact) or synthesis-inclusion (judges findings against a freshly synthesized artifact, no source snapshot).

prompt_version.description: Identifier of the survival-classifier.md prompt revision that produced this row.

finding_id.description: Joins back to the prior stage's findings.jsonl row by finding_id.

outcome.description: addressed: revision changed the artifact in a way that addresses the finding (addressed-by-revision mode), or design recommendation visibly shaped the synthesized artifact (synthesis-inclusion mode). not_addressed: no visible change / Judge dropped the recommendation. rejected_intentionally: revised artifact's Open Questions / Out of Scope / Backlog Routing / Deferred section explicitly names this finding (case-insensitive header match). classifier_error: classifier failed (LLM error, malformed output, context overflow); evidence carries the error reason, confidence is 'low'.

evidence.description: Verbatim quote from the (revised) artifact, ≤120 Unicode codepoints. Literal 'no change' when outcome == 'not_addressed'. Validator: each non-'no change' quote must appear as a substring of the artifact (after NFC normalization on both sides), else outcome demoted to 'not_addressed' and confidence demoted to 'low'. Note: maxLength is in chars (codepoints), not bytes; 240 is a generous byte-level upper bound.

confidence.description: Classifier certainty. 'low' is preferred over guessing when the artifact is ambiguous about a finding.

artifact_hash.description: sha256 of the (revised) artifact at classification time. Used for idempotency: on re-run, if current sha256 matches recorded hash, skip; if differs, re-classify and overwrite. Fixture format 'sha256:fixture-*' is allowed for test artifacts.
